metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that S2's spec is amended to add topic/`stack_id` assignment at selection time (adding `stack_id` to the `wipChain` schema or as a parallel output), so S3's assertion that S2 supplies this key is backed by S2's own AC.
findings.finding: S3 asserts that `stack_id` is 'the Gerrit topic the auto-lander assigns to a stack at selection time' and is 'the shared key across S2/S3/S4/S9', but S2's ticket (the dependency) has no `stack_id` field in its `wipChain` schema, no topic-assignment step in any AC, and zero occurrences of `stack_id` appear anywhere in the repo. S3 relies on a capability S2 does not specify.
findings.location: Outcome record section: '`stack_id` = the Gerrit topic the auto-lander assigns to a stack at selection time (falling back to the tip change-id for a single-change stack); it is the shared key across S2/S3/S4/S9.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S2's AC: 'At selection time, assign a `stack_id` to the `wipChain`: use the Gerrit topic if one exists on the tip change, else use the tip `change_id` as a string; record `stack_id` in the `wipChain` schema alongside `change_id`.' Then S3's assertion becomes verifiable against S2's own spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the outside-dir infrastructure files that must be updated to host the autolander state dir: `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` (new volume), `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` SITE_SUBDIRS (new bind-mount entry), and `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` (drift-gate pairing) — or explicitly state how the state dir is persisted without these changes.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new auto-lander service with a persistent 'project-infra state dir' for the outcome record store, but does not enumerate the outside-dir infrastructure files that hosting a new service/volume would require updating: `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` (new service or volume entry), `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` SITE_SUBDIRS list (new state dir bind-volume), and `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` (drift-gate pairing check). These are all outside `infra/autolander/` and are load-bearing for the state dir to actually persist across container restarts.
findings.location: Outcome record section: 'auto-lander state dir (project-infra state)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `autolander` to SITE_SUBDIRS in compose-up.sh, declare a `gerrit_autolander` external volume in docker-compose.yml mounted at the state dir path, and note these files in the plan's edit-target list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `stack_id` to the S2 `wipChain` schema and specify its assignment rule (Gerrit topic at selection time, fallback to tip change-id) co-located with the S2 selection AC, so S3's outcome-record writer has a defined source for the upsert key.
findings.finding: The `stack_id` assignment protocol (topic assigned at selection time, fallback to tip change-id) is specified in S3's Outcome record section, but the selection logic lives in S2's `wipChain` — S2's schema (`{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase}`) contains no `stack_id` field and no topic-assignment step. The decision rule for how and when `stack_id` is set is not co-located with the schema that needs it (wipChain in S2), creating a structural gap for any executor implementing the cross-story contract.
findings.location: Outcome record section ('`stack_id` = the Gerrit topic the auto-lander assigns to a stack at selection time'); S2 wipChain schema
findings.suggested_fix: In S2's wipChain schema AC, add `stack_id` as a field set during selection (before rebasing phase), with the same fallback rule currently stated only in S3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit population rules for `failing_change_id?` co-located with the outcome record schema: specify it as the change whose patchset received `Verified -1` (for `ci_failed`) and the change whose `POST /rebase` returned 409 (for `needs_rebase`).
findings.finding: The `failing_change_id?` population rule is never stated for either outcome type. For `ci_failed` the failing change is the one whose patchset received `Verified -1`, but the plan never says how to identify it when a chain has multiple members. For `needs_rebase` the plan says outcome is 'event-driven on that change/stack' implying the 409-receiving change is `failing_change_id`, but this is implicit. Neither rule is co-located with the schema definition.
findings.location: Outcome record section: `failing_change_id?`; AC-1 (CI-fail); AC-2 (rebase-conflict)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Outcome record section, annotate: `failing_change_id?` — for `ci_failed`: the change_id of the patchset that received Verified -1; for `needs_rebase`: the change_id whose POST /rebase returned 409; null/absent if not applicable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify Gerrit's actual HTTP response codes for `POST /changes/{id}/revisions/{revId}/review` when a label is already absent or the change is already merged/abandoned, and update the AC to list only the codes that Gerrit actually returns (likely `200` for already-absent, `404` for unknown change — not `409`).
findings.finding: The Idempotent ordering AC states 'label-removal treats 404/409 as a no-op', but Gerrit's label-removal endpoint (POST /changes/{id}/revisions/{revId}/review with a zero-vote payload) does not return 409 when a label is already absent — it returns 200. The 409 is the rebase-conflict status code, not a label-removal code. This unverified assertion may conflate the two paths and could cause a real 409 from a concurrent submit/rebase attempt during label removal to be silently swallowed rather than surfaced.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Idempotent ordering under retries/restart (last bullet)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the actual HTTP status codes POST /revisions/{rev}/review (setReview) returns on error (especially: does it ever return 409?), and replace the blanket '404/409 no-op' rule with the confirmed codes — likely only 404 (change/revision not found) is the idempotent case for label removal.
findings.finding: The idempotency AC asserts 'label-removal treats 404/409 as a no-op', but POST /revisions/{rev}/review (setReview) does not return 409 on a label-removal call — 409 is the response code for POST /rebase on a conflict. Treating a hypothetical setReview 409 as a no-op is based on an unverified/contradicted assumption about that endpoint's error surface; if setReview does return 409 (e.g. for a concurrent edit conflict on Gerrit's internal locking), swallowing it silently could mask a real failure during Autosubmit removal.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Idempotent ordering under retries/restart: 'label-removal treats 404/409 as a no-op'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC to: 'label-removal treats `404` (change/revision not found — already gone) as a no-op; other non-2xx responses are logged and retried per the retry policy.' Remove the unsupported 409 assertion for setReview.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `tested_sha` value for the `needs_rebase` outcome (e.g., null/absent since no rebase succeeded), co-located with the outcome record schema definition.
findings.finding: The `tested_sha` field in the outcome record schema is undefined for the `needs_rebase` failure mode. In the `ci_failed` case the tested SHA is the post-rebase patchset SHA that received the `-1`. But in the `needs_rebase` case (409 from `POST /rebase`), no successful rebase has occurred, so no post-rebase SHA exists. The plan never states what value `tested_sha` holds in this case (null, absent, or the pre-rebase SHA), leaving the schema incomplete for one of its two outcome values.
findings.location: Outcome record section: `{stack_id, change_ids[], outcome ∈ {needs_rebase, ci_failed}, failing_change_id?, tested_sha, recorded_at, acknowledged}`
findings.suggested_fix: Annotate the schema: `tested_sha` — the post-rebase patchset SHA; present for `ci_failed`, null/omitted for `needs_rebase`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the storage medium and on-disk schema for the outcome record store, and explicitly assert the store's state after a rollback (including whether residual `acknowledged=false` records are safe, require cleanup, or are compensated-forward) — attach a `DATA LOSS RISK` note if partial-write or rollback can leave records in an incorrect intermediate state.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new persisted record store ('project-infra state dir', keyed by stack_id) but never specifies the storage medium, the on-disk schema, or the state of that store after a rollback. If S3 is deployed, writes acknowledged=false records, and is then rolled back, the pre-S3 service has no awareness of those records; the plan does not assert what schema state exists post-rollback or whether residual records can cause harm (e.g., S4/S9 reading stale acknowledged=false records on a future re-deploy).
findings.location: Outcome record section; AC5 (idempotent ordering under retries/restart)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a rollback note to the Outcome record section, e.g.: 'Rolling back S3 to pre-S3 code does not delete existing records. Any acknowledged=false record left by the rolled-back service is inert until S3 is re-deployed, at which point it re-drives label-removal idempotently. Operators may optionally delete the state dir to suppress re-drive on next deploy.' Also specify the storage format (e.g., one JSON file per stack_id under <state_dir>/outcomes/<stack_id>.json) so rollback behavior is unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add S9's ticket ID/alias to the plan (or explicitly note it is a future story), and define the S3↔S9 contract boundary (the record fields S9 will read) so the schema is frozen against a real consumer.
findings.finding: S9 is named as a downstream consumer of the outcome record ('S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this record as the authoritative outcome') but S9 has no ticket alias, ticket ID, or any file/symbol footprint in the repository. It cannot be verified as a real planned story, and no contract boundary between S3's record and S9's read path is specified.
findings.location: Outcome record section: 'S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this record'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit `file_impact` / edit-target paths to S3 (e.g. `infra/autolander/loop.py` for failure branches, plus any new outcome-store module path like `infra/autolander/outcome_store.py`) so the edit set is verifiable.
findings.finding: S3 names no concrete file path for its own edit targets. The plan says 'failure/edge paths of the core-loop service' and 'project-infra state dir' but never specifies which file(s) it will create or modify (e.g. infra/autolander/loop.py, a new infra/autolander/failure_handler.py, an outcome store module, etc.). Without a named path, the G1G2 'every file/symbol named exists' check cannot be performed and implementers have no grounded edit target.
findings.location: Scope section; Outcome record section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `file_impact` section naming at minimum `infra/autolander/loop.py` (failure-branch additions) and `infra/autolander/outcome_store.py` (or equivalent) for the keyed record store.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `ci_failed` detection must be gated on the `Verified -1` being on the current post-rebase patchset SHA (`tested_sha`), mirroring S2's 'exact rebased patchset SHA' precision for the `+1` detection path.
findings.finding: The CI-fail detection AC says 'post-rebase `Verified -1`' but does not require the -1 to be on the current post-rebase patchset SHA (`tested_sha`), unlike S2's explicit 'fresh Verified +1 on the exact rebased patchset SHA' definition. An implementer polling Gerrit's label state immediately after a rebase could theoretically read label metadata in a transitional state, and more practically, the asymmetry leaves the implementer without clear guidance on whether a stale pre-rebase -1 vote (if somehow present) should trigger `ci_failed`. The plan should mirror S2's SHA-pinning requirement for the -1 case.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — CI-fail bullet: 'on a post-rebase `Verified -1`'
findings.suggested_fix: In the CI-fail AC, change 'on a post-rebase `Verified -1`' to 'on a `Verified -1` cast against the exact post-rebase patchset SHA (`tested_sha`), not a copied/carried vote from a prior patchset' — mirroring S2's fresh-Verified definition.

findings.finding: The `wipChain` phase after a failure event is never specified. S2 defines `phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle}`. S3 adds CI-fail and 409-conflict branches to S2's loop but does not state which phase the `wipChain` enters after the outcome is recorded — whether it transitions to `idle`, is cleared, or requires a new phase value like `failed`. Without this, the executor cannot determine S2's loop's next-iteration behavior after S3's failure handling completes.
findings.location: AC-1 (CI-fail path), AC-2 (rebase-conflict path); cross-ref S2 wipChain schema (phase field)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC-1 and AC-2: 'After label removal completes, set `wipChain.phase = idle` and clear `wipChain.change_id` so the next loop iteration re-enters the `selecting` phase.' If a new phase value is needed, amend S2's `phase` enum explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design doc) capturing the outcome record schema, last-write-wins semantics, `acknowledged` flag lifecycle, `stack_id` derivation rule, and producer/consumer obligations for S3/S4/S9.
findings.finding: The outcome record is a novel cross-component contract (shared by S3, S4, S9) and constitutes a new pipeline stage, but the plan names no documentation artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design-doc update) that captures its schema, semantics (last-write-wins upsert, acknowledged flag lifecycle, stack_id derivation rule), and consumer obligations.
findings.location: Outcome record section; Acceptance Criteria (all ACs implicitly depend on this contract)
findings.suggested_fix: Create a short ADR (or a `## Outcome Record Contract` section in CLAUDE.md / project-infra design doc) that specifies: (1) the full record schema with field semantics, (2) the last-write-wins upsert rule and why (idempotent re-runs), (3) the `acknowledged` flag state machine (false on write → true after label removal completes), (4) the `stack_id` derivation rule (topic → tip change-id fallback), and (5) which stories produce vs consume the record.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE/cleanup path for outcome records: specify when a record is deleted or marked superseded (e.g., on successful stack landing, or after the agent sets a remediated flag), and how S4/S9 distinguish a current failure record from a stale one.
findings.finding: The plan defines no RETIRE/cleanup path for outcome records in the keyed store. Records accumulate indefinitely per stack_id; there is no specification of when a record is deleted (e.g., after successful re-land, after N days, after agent acknowledges remediation).
findings.location: Outcome record section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the outcome record store should reuse or adapt `DedupStore`'s sqlite3 keyed-upsert pattern (`src/rebar/review_bot/dedup.py`) or justify a fresh implementation (e.g. different service boundary, different schema needs); document the decision explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan describes a new 'keyed record store' with last-write-wins SQLite upsert semantics for the autolander outcome record, but the codebase already has a production implementation of exactly this pattern: `DedupStore` in `src/rebar/review_bot/dedup.py` (sqlite3, `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`, keyed by a compound key, idempotent upsert, stdlib-only). The plan gives no signal whether to reuse this pattern/code or reinvent it for the autolander service, risking an accidental NIH re-implementation.
findings.location: Outcome record section; 'keyed record store … last-write-wins on upsert'
findings.suggested_fix: If the autolander is a fully separate service with no import path to `src/rebar/review_bot/`, an inline sqlite3 upsert is fine — but add a one-line comment citing DedupStore as the precedent so future readers don't treat it as novel infrastructure.

findings.finding: The plan makes multiple Gerrit REST calls on behalf of `autolander-bot` (label removal via `POST /revisions/{rev}/review`, at minimum one call per stack member) but states no auth/HTTPS preconditions: which HTTP token is used, how it is provisioned, where it is stored (SSM path), and how it is injected into the autolander service at runtime. The `Autosubmit` label vote ACL (from S1) grants `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1` only to `Autosubmit Voters` (containing `autolander-bot`) + Administrators. The existing review-bot uses `BOT_USER:GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` for `rebar-review-bot` — a separate identity. The autolander service needs a distinct `autolander-bot` HTTP token, provisioned and rotatable, but the plan is entirely silent on this.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — CI-fail item ('remove Autosubmit from all changes in the affected stack'), Rebase-conflict item ('remove Autosubmit from all changes in the stack'), Idempotent ordering item ('label-removal treats 404/409 as a no-op')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Auth preconditions' section: 'The autolander service authenticates to Gerrit REST as `autolander-bot` using HTTP Basic auth (`autolander-bot:<AUTOLANDER_BOT_TOKEN>`). The token is provisioned by `infra/gerrit/autolander-service-user.sh` (new, mirrors `service-user.sh`) and stored at SSM path `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token`, injected as `AUTOLANDER_BOT_TOKEN` at runtime. S1 must merge and provision the `autolander-bot` Gerrit account before S3 can be deployed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete verification step for each AC — at minimum: (a) a test-scenario description (real or injected-fault) that exercises the CI-fail and 409 paths against a Gerrit instance or a faithful integration test harness; (b) a command (e.g. `jq -e '.outcome == "ci_failed" and .acknowledged == false' <state-dir>/<stack_id>.json`) that asserts the record was written correctly; (c) a Gerrit query (e.g. `ssh gerrit gerrit query --format JSON change:<id> --current-patch-set | jq '.currentPatchSet.approvals[] | select(.type=="Autosubmit")'`) confirming label removal; and (d) a restart-and-re-drive scenario for AC5.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion maps to a concrete proving command, step, or verification procedure. The plan describes WHAT each failure mode must do but provides no described implementation steps and no test/check that would produce evidence of correctness for any of the five ACs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five bullets)
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a `## Verification` section with one proving step per AC, e.g.:
- AC1/AC2: integration test that (i) rebases a synthetic stack, (ii) injects a Verified -1 / 409, (iii) asserts `jq -e '.outcome=="ci_failed" and .acknowledged==false'` on the state file immediately after, and (iv) asserts Autosubmit label absent on all changes via Gerrit REST or SSH query.
- AC5: kill the service after first label removal, restart it, then assert all remaining labels were also removed (idempotent re-drive).
- AC4: assert the Gerrit rebase call is never issued with `allow_conflicts=true` (grep the implementation or assert via test spy).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete record store format and file naming convention (e.g. 'one JSON file per stack, path `<auto-lander-state-dir>/outcomes/<stack_id>.json`, atomically written via rename') so S4/S9 consumers can rely on the same layout.
findings.finding: The persistence format and location of the 'keyed record store' is unspecified — the executor must choose between JSON files, SQLite, a key-value store, or another mechanism. 'auto-lander state dir (project-infra state)' names a directory but not a file format or naming convention.
findings.location: Outcome record section: 'The bot writes to a keyed record store in the auto-lander state dir'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Outcome record section: 'Stored as one UTF-8 JSON file per stack at `<auto-lander-state-dir>/outcomes/<stack_id>.json`; written atomically via a temp-file rename to prevent torn reads by S4/S9 consumers.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly how the post-rebase Verified -1 is detected (e.g. 'the S2 loop re-checks the CI vote by polling `GET /changes/{id}/detail` every N seconds up to a timeout; S3 branches when that vote equals -1'), or add a cross-reference to the S2 section that defines it.
findings.finding: The trigger/detection mechanism for a post-rebase Verified -1 is not specified in this story. It is unclear whether the bot polls Gerrit for the CI vote after issuing a rebase, subscribes to a Gerrit event stream, or is notified via a webhook — yet that choice determines the latency, complexity, and correctness of the CI-fail branch.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'on a post-rebase Verified -1, record a ci_failed outcome'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence: 'CI vote detection is delegated to the S2 loop (which polls/streams the vote on the tip change after each rebase); S3 branches on the `Verified=-1` result from that mechanism — no additional polling is introduced here.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ACs (or extend AC5) to verify: (a) writer sets `acknowledged=false` at record time and `true` after all Autosubmit removals complete; (b) `failing_change_id` is populated on `ci_failed` outcomes; (c) `tested_sha` records the SHA under test at the time of the failure.
findings.finding: Three fields described in the Outcome record schema — `acknowledged` (write mechanics: set false on record, set true after removal completes), `tested_sha`, and `failing_change_id?` — have no covering acceptance criterion that verifies they are correctly populated or used.
findings.location: Outcome record section; all ACs
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC5 with: 'The writer sets acknowledged=false immediately on upsert; after all label-removal calls succeed (or 404/409), the record is updated to acknowledged=true. On ci_failed outcomes, failing_change_id is the change whose Verified vote is -1; tested_sha is the HEAD SHA of the rebased stack at the time CI was polled.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/backoff (e.g., exponential backoff with a cap) and a timeout for Gerrit API calls (POST /rebase, label-removal), and define how 5xx/network errors are surfaced vs. retried vs. circuit-broken.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no retry/backoff for Gerrit API calls (POST /rebase, label-removal). A transient Gerrit outage during label removal leaves the stack in an unacknowledged=false limbo until the next restart, with no bounded retry interval or circuit-breaker to prevent thundering-herd on recovery.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — idempotent ordering bullet; Outcome record section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC bullet: 'Gerrit API calls use exponential backoff (e.g., 1s/2s/4s, max 3 retries); 5xx and network errors are logged as warnings and re-attempted on the next restart cycle; a per-stack error count is emitted as a metric.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: specify that ci_failed and needs_rebase outcomes are emitted as structured log events and/or counters, and that unacknowledged records older than a threshold trigger an alert or are surfaced in a dashboard.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no observability (metrics, structured logs, or alerts) for any of the new failure paths — CI-fail, rebase-conflict, Gerrit API errors, or record-store write failures — leaving operators blind to failure rates and stuck stacks.
findings.location: Entire plan — no instrumentation section or AC bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the acknowledged flag is updated atomically (e.g., via a conditional write/CAS checking expected previous value, or with an instance-held lease) to prevent two concurrent instances from both marking a record acknowledged before all removals are confirmed complete.
findings.finding: The two-step write to the acknowledged flag (set false on record, set true after all removals complete) is not specified as atomic or lock-protected, creating a TOCTOU race if two auto-lander instances run concurrently (e.g., overlapping restart).
findings.location: Outcome record section — 'acknowledged' field description; Acceptance Criteria — idempotent ordering bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a testing plan (or test ACs) covering at minimum: (a) CI-fail happy path and restart-with-unacknowledged-record, (b) 409 rebase-conflict path, (c) 404/409-as-no-op on label removal, and (d) single-change stack key fallback — specifying whether each is covered by a unit, integration, or end-to-end test and identifying any live Gerrit boundary that must be exercised rather than mocked.
findings.finding: The plan introduces substantial new logic — CI-fail detection, rebase-conflict (409) handling, outcome record upsert, Autosubmit bulk-removal, and idempotent restart re-driving — but contains zero testing plan: no unit tests, integration tests, or end-to-end acceptance criteria that specify how any of these paths are exercised. The idempotent-restart path (unacknowledged=false re-drives label removal on boot) is especially complex and entirely untested by the plan. Because these are new user-facing flows that agents depend on as a contract, the absence of any failure-path test specification is a MAJOR gap under E5.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) and Outcome record section — no testing section exists anywhere in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Testing' section with ACs of the form: 'A simulated Verified -1 after rebase causes outcome record ci_failed to be written before any label-removal call, and a subsequent restart with acknowledged=false re-issues label removal for all changes (idempotent)'; similarly for 409 and for the single-change key fallback. Flag the outcome-record write + label-removal sequence as requiring an integration test (not purely mocked) so the acknowledged handshake is proven correct against a real or realistic record store.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the keyed record store design in the Outcome record section (e.g., why last-write-wins over append-only, why local file store over Gerrit-native signaling) so implementers and reviewers can verify alignment.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no positive rationale for why a keyed record store (last-write-wins upsert, one file per `stack_id`) was chosen as the outcome persistence mechanism. The 'What'/'Outcome record' section describes HOW the store works but contains no statement of WHY this design fits the constraints (e.g., single-instance service, local filesystem already used for bot state, S4/S9 need synchronous file reads without Gerrit API dependency). The absence of a stated rationale leaves implementers without context to make compatible implementation decisions and violates the G6 positive-rationale requirement.
findings.location: Outcome record section (whole section)
findings.suggested_fix: Add after the store description: 'Rationale: a single per-stack file (keyed by `stack_id`) on the local state volume gives atomic upsert via rename, is readable by S4/S9 without a live Gerrit connection, and avoids log-compaction complexity — the single current failure state per stack is all consumers need.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief justification in the Outcome record section explaining why a new keyed mutable store is preferred over (a) writing a structured event to the existing rebar ticket/event system or (b) a simpler per-change Gerrit mechanism, referencing ADR-0040 R6's 'proven OSS workflow' requirement.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel keyed, last-write-wins mutable record store ('project-infra state, NOT a rebar event') as a shared cross-component contract between S3, S4, and S9, but provides no justification for why a custom store is preferred over the existing rebar event/ticket system or a simpler mechanism (e.g., writing a structured comment/event to the existing ticket, or persisting the wipChain to disk). ADR-0040 R6 requires emulating proven OSS workflows rather than inventing new ones; the outcome-record contract is an invented artifact whose novelty vs. alternatives is unevaluated.
findings.location: Outcome record section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph rationale, e.g.: 'A rebar ticket event is not used because the auto-lander outcome is ephemeral bot-internal state (overwritten per re-run, not append-only), and writing/reading rebar events would couple the project-infra service to the rebar store's locking/replay path. A per-change Gerrit mechanism (hashtag, topic) is not used because the outcome must be readable without a Gerrit API call at S4/S9 decision time. A flat keyed file store under the service's state dir gives last-write-wins upsert semantics with a single fsync, matching the ADR-0040 "lightweight bot" intent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether GET /changes/{id} returns owner._account_id without any extra option flag (it is typically always present in ChangeInfo.owner), and if so add a note to the plan; if DETAILED_ACCOUNTS is needed, name that option explicitly in the AC.
findings.finding: The plan identifies the stack owner via 'owner._account_id on the tip change' but does not specify which Gerrit query option (e.g. DETAILED_ACCOUNTS) is required to guarantee the owner object is populated with _account_id in the GET /changes/{id} response. Without the correct option flag the owner object may be returned as a stub (account_id only, no further detail) or may be absent, making the owner identification precondition unverified at the endpoint-surface level.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Stack → owner: 'owning agent is the stack's uploader/owner (owner._account_id on the tip change)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that a 404 or 409 response from label-removal counts as successful completion for the purpose of setting `acknowledged=true` (i.e., 'completes' means all changes have been attempted with 2xx, 404, or 409 responses).
findings.finding: The `acknowledged=true` transition rule is ambiguous when all label-removal calls return 404/409 (all treated as no-ops). AC-5 says '404/409 = no-op', and the Outcome record section says `acknowledged=true` is set 'after the `Autosubmit`-removal for every change in the stack completes'. It is unspecified whether a stack where every removal returned 404 (label already gone) still counts as 'completes' — i.e., whether no-op-success constitutes completion for the acknowledged flag.
findings.location: Outcome record section (`acknowledged` field); AC-5 ('label-removal treats `404`/`409` as a no-op')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the Outcome record section: 'sets it `true` after attempting `Autosubmit`-removal for every change in the stack, where 2xx, 404, and 409 all count as completion.'

findings.finding: The `acknowledged` field in the outcome record has an undefined write-back owner: the plan says 'on restart an unacknowledged record re-drives label removal to completion' but never specifies what sets acknowledged=true, when, or which story is responsible — an executor implementing this story cannot complete AC5 without guessing.
findings.location: Outcome record section; AC5 (Idempotent ordering under retries/restart)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Outcome record section: 'The auto-lander loop sets `acknowledged = true` immediately after all label-removal calls for the stack have completed (200, 404, or 409 treated as success). S4/S9 only read this field; they do not write it.'

findings.finding: `stack_id` is used as the upsert key for the outcome record and is referenced as the cross-story SSOT consumed by S4 and S9, but is never defined anywhere in the plan. S2's wipChain schema contains no `stack_id` field. Without a definition, neither its derivation rule (tip change_id? root change_id? hash of chain_member_ids[]?) nor its stability guarantee across re-runs is knowable, breaking the idempotency and cross-story consumption mechanism.
findings.location: Outcome record section; AC5 ('upsert-keyed on `stack_id`'); Dependencies (S2 wipChain schema)
findings.suggested_fix: Define stack_id as the Gerrit change number of the stack's root (bottom-most) change in the relation chain, sourced from wipChain.chain_member_ids[0]. This is stable: the root never changes identity even as the chain is rebased, and it is derivable from S2's existing schema without a new concept.

findings.finding: The `acknowledged` field in the outcome record schema has no write-protocol specified: the plan never states who sets it, what the initial value is, what event or actor transitions it from false→true, or where that transition code lives. The only reference is in the restart AC ('an unacknowledged record re-drives'), which assumes the field exists and has a known transition point, but the decision rule for writing it is absent from the schema definition.
findings.location: Outcome record section (schema definition) and AC-5 (idempotent ordering under retries/restart)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Outcome record section, add: '`acknowledged` is written exclusively by the lander loop; it is set `false` on record creation and flipped to `true` only after every `Autosubmit` removal for the stack has received a 2xx or no-op 404/409. S4 and S9 are read-only consumers and MUST NOT write this field.'

findings.finding: The `stack_id` field is used as the upsert key throughout the schema and consumed by S4 and S9, but its derivation rule is never defined — no formula (e.g., derived from tip change id, hash of change_ids[], Gerrit topic name) is specified anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Outcome record section: '{stack_id, change_ids[], outcome ∈ {needs_rebase, ci_failed}, failing_change_id?, tested_sha, recorded_at, acknowledged}' and AC 5 upsert-key clause

findings.finding: The `acknowledged` field's lifecycle is ambiguous: the plan states S3 writes it into the outcome record and that 'an unacknowledged record re-drives the label removal to completion on restart', but it never specifies (a) what value S3 assigns on first write (`false`? omitted?), (b) what constitutes acknowledgment (label removal fully applied? S4/S9 consumption?), and (c) which component sets it to `true`. An executor must guess whether S3 owns both the write and the flip, or whether S4/S9 flip it after consuming the record.
findings.location: Outcome record section; AC5 ('on restart an unacknowledged record re-drives the label removal to completion')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Outcome record section: '`acknowledged` (bool, default `false`): set to `true` by S3 once every label-removal in the stack has completed (2xx/404/409). S4 and S9 read but never write this field; they treat any record regardless of `acknowledged` value as authoritative for the outcome type.'

findings.finding: The `acknowledged` field is declared in the record schema and referenced in AC5's restart-recovery rule ('on restart an unacknowledged record re-drives the label removal to completion'), but no processing protocol specifies what sets `acknowledged` to true, who sets it, and at what point in the workflow.
findings.location: Outcome record section (field `acknowledged`) and AC5 idempotent-ordering bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Outcome record section: '`acknowledged` is set to `true` by S3 only, after all `Autosubmit` label-removal calls for every `change_ids[]` entry have returned 200, 404, or 409. S4 and S9 treat `acknowledged=false` as "removal may be incomplete" and MUST NOT set this field.'

findings.finding: The `acknowledged` field is described in the Outcome record schema and its read path is covered by the idempotency AC ('on restart an unacknowledged record re-drives the label removal to completion'), but no AC specifies when or how `acknowledged` is set to `true` — its write path is a described deliverable with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Outcome record section (field `acknowledged`) + Idempotency AC
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the idempotency AC: '...once all label removals complete (or return 404/409 no-ops), the record is updated to `acknowledged: true`; subsequent restarts skip label removal for acknowledged stacks.'

findings.finding: The 'auto-lander state dir' outcome record is described as both 'append-only JSON' and 'upsert-keyed on stack_id (re-run overwrites prior record)' — these are contradictory storage semantics. The plan introduces a novel persistence pattern (mutable upsert file outside rebar's event-sourced store) with no prior-art consideration of alternatives (append-only + projection, SQLite, in-memory + restart-recovery log) and no justification for this specific choice.
findings.location: Outcome record section: 'The bot writes an append-only JSON record … upsert-keyed on stack_id (a re-run of the same stack overwrites its prior record) — NOT one blind append per attempt.'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove 'append-only' from the description (it is wrong for an upsert). Add a one-sentence justification: e.g., 'A single mutable JSON file per stack_id is chosen over an append-only log because the record is the authoritative current state (not a history), and S4/S9 need only the latest outcome without replay; recovery from a partial write is handled by the idempotent restart path (unacknowledged record re-drives label removal).' If append semantics are actually intended, switch to a log with a projection query keyed on stack_id.

findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple new behavioral paths — CI-fail detection, 409/rebase-conflict handling, bulk Autosubmit removal, and idempotent restart re-drive — but specifies no testing approach whatsoever. There are no unit tests, integration tests, or acceptance-test scenarios named anywhere in the plan. The failure paths (CI-fail, 409) are themselves the new user-facing flows, and they have zero test coverage specified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five bullets); Outcome record section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a '## Testing' section listing: (1) a parametrized test covering both outcome types (ci_failed / needs_rebase) asserting the outcome record is written before any Gerrit label call; (2) a test that after a simulated mid-run restart the unacknowledged record causes label removal to be retried; (3) a test that a stack with N changes has Autosubmit removed from all N (not just the failing one); (4) a test that allow_conflicts is never passed to POST /rebase.

findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple new user-facing failure flows (CI-fail path, 409 rebase-conflict path, idempotent restart/recovery path, stack-atomic Autosubmit removal) but contains zero testing specification — no unit, integration, or end-to-end test strategy is described anywhere. The ACs state desired behavior but do not constitute a test plan. Critically, the sequencing invariant 'outcome recorded BEFORE Autosubmit removal' and the partial-failure scenario (label removal fails mid-stack, then service restarts) have no adversarial test coverage called out.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section (all ACs); 'Idempotent ordering under retries/restart' AC bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Testing' section to the plan with: (1) Unit tests: outcome record upsert-on-same-stack_id, label-removal 404/409 treated as no-op, CI-fail and 409 routing logic. (2) Integration test (Gerrit sandbox or recorded-replay): POST /rebase 409 → outcome written before any label call → all Autosubmit labels removed → restart with unacknowledged record → labels removed again idempotently. (3) Fault-injection test: simulate label-removal failure on change N-of-M mid-stack, assert restart completes remaining removals without duplicating the outcome record.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
